Line 3.5 (Impacts of AI on Employment p. 51) establishes a specific line of action to address AI-driven job displacement including studies on labour market impacts programmes to maintain employability for both active and inactive workers and engagement with employers and workers' organisations to identify human capital development opportunities.
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).

fr1_title: National Artificial Intelligence Strategy of Uruguay 2024-2030
fr1_justif: Uruguay’s National AI Strategy explicitly incorporates the principles of safety security reliability and responsible innovation. The Strategy dedicates guiding principles to “Reliability and Security” (p. 32) and “Responsible and Safe Innovation” (p. 33) establishes a governance framework to ensure ethical responsible and secure AI (p. 21–22) and contains a cybersecurity section (p. 44). These provisions collectively align with the accuracy reliability and risk-management mechanisms.
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).

fr1_title: National Artificial Intelligence Strategy of Uruguay 2024-2030
fr1_justif: Principle 6 is Transparency and Explainability: (page 31)Transparency and explainability play a crucial role in ensuring the effectiveness of all the above principles. AI stakeholders must ensure transparency and explainability in relation to AI systems. This involves providing meaningful information appropriate to the context and the specific situation balanced with the protection of other rights that may be involved. The information disclosed must make the results intelligible and must be appropriate to enable people to challenge them when they are affected. In addition transparency is specifically linked to strengthening democracy (page 26) regulatory frameworks (page 37) and public management (page 49).
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).

fr1_title: Resolution of the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan on the Approval of the Strategy for the Development of Artificial Intelligence Technologies until 2030
fr1_justif: Chapter 3 section 5 promotes enhancing knowledge and skills of the population and developing human resources. Chapter 3 par.3 introduces game-based and experimental learning for early ages.  promotes AI learning “for youth and across all segments”. Par. 5 of Chapter 3 includes measures to improve teacher/professor skills (foreign training upskilling programmes); establish AI labs at universities; provide online courses with foreign experts; build training systems for school and university staff. However it provides no specific measures targeting  measures targeting: rural schools low-income students gender gaps people with disabilities.
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_body_name: Coordination Commission for the Implementation of the Digital Uzbekistan 2030 Strategy

fr1_title: Draft Law on the protection of the rights of users of online platforms and websites (Uzbekistan)
fr1_justif: The draft addresses AI-facilitated misinformation and violence through Article 12 (?ontent moderation) which obliges online-platform owners to: (1) strengthen content-moderation systems and (2) ensure AI technologies operate in compliance with the Law. These duties directly target AI-driven harms by requiring platforms to improve detection/removal workflows and to govern their AI tools accordingly.
fr1_scope: Vertical approach (the framework targets specific sectors specific types of data or  AI technologies.

fr1_title: Resolution of the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan on the Approval of the Strategy for the Development of Artificial Intelligence Technologies until 2030
fr1_justif: The Strategy does not contain any direct references to cultural or linguistic diversity multilingual AI localization or the protection of minority languages. However the Strategy outlines framework for the development of a National AI Language Model that takes into account the linguistic characteristics of the region. Par. 4 states 'forming specialized datasets for tasks such as voice video and communication analysis in the Uzbek language'.
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_body_name: Coordination Commission for the Implementation of the Digital Uzbekistan 2030 Strategy

fr1_title: Draft Ethical Rules for Artificial Intelligence Development and Usage (Uzbekistan)
fr1_justif: The Draft ethical rules addresses Fairness and Non-discrimination by defining key risks and required safeguards. It explains that AI systems must avoid biased or discriminatory outcomes noting that algorithmic bias arises from underrepresented or flawed data and can disproportionately harm protected groups (p.1–2) . It highlights the human-rights basis for non-discrimination and stresses preventing AI from amplifying societal inequalities (p.2–3). The document identifies measures such as bias audits fairness metrics mitigation duties and rights to explanation and redress (pp.3–5).
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).

fr1_title: Draft Ethical Rules for Artificial Intelligence Development and Usage (Uzbekistan)
fr1_justif: The document treats Impact Assessments mainly as pre-deployment and ongoing checks. P.4 §6 Rights & Obligations of AI Developers/Implementers requires developers to “organize assessments and tests” to detect/mitigate bias and to document system limits/potential impacts. On p.5 Part III it §8 Internal Control & Monitoring implies risk identification + safety-measure effectiveness recommended . §9 annual External Audit recommended and §10 Risk Assessment before implementation; includes human-rights impact bias/discrimination safety privacy/security threats.
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).

indicator: Public Disclosure of Government Algorithmic Systems

fr1_title: Strategy for the Development of Artificial Intelligence Technologies until 2030 (Uzbekistan)
fr1_justif: The Strategy addresses public-sector skills development by requiring government bodies to strengthen AI competencies among civil servants. Chapter 3 sets the objective to enhance skills in the use of AI technologies digital skills data analytics and big data for specialists and public employees. Paragraph 5 of Chapter 3 expands training through AI laboratories programming and machine-learning modules and professional retraining programmes for personnel involved in digital-government tasks. Appendix 2 assigns ministries concrete tasks to deliver AI training for staff.
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr2_title: On Measures to Radically Improve the System of Training Highly Qualified Personnel in the Field of Justice
fr2_justif: The decree addresses public-sector skills development in the judiciary by embedding AI-related competencies into judicial training. Section I(1)(d) requires candidates judges and court staff to acquire skills in the “practical application of modern information technologies and the capabilities of artificial intelligence.” Section III(6)(i) expands professional retraining through virtual court modules and classes on using AI technologies. Section VI(18) authorises use of the Adolat digital systems complex to support AI-enabled learning and research
fr2_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).

fr1_title: Resolution of the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan on the Approval of the Strategy for the Development of Artificial Intelligence Technologies until 2030
fr1_justif: The Strategy addresses reskilling and upskilling by prioritizing AI-skills development across education and the workforce. Chapter 3 sets the objective to “enhance the knowledge and skills of the population in the use of AI technologies” and expand human-resource capacity . It calls for teaching programming and AI technologies to students and improving the competencies of teachers and specialists. Chapter 5 introduces AI labs at universities and foreign training programs and expands access to online AI courses and professional retraining to prepare workers for an AI-driven economy.
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_body_name: Coordination Commission for the Implementation of the Digital Uzbekistan 2030 Strategy

fr1_title: Draft Ethical Rules for Artificial Intelligence Development and Usage (Uzbekistan)
fr1_justif: The Rules directly addresses transparency and explainability through Section 4.6 which requires AI systems to produce clear understandable and explainable outcomes and obliges developers to ensure transparency of training databases including sources limitations and potential biases.Section 6 further obliges developers to ensure transparency and understandability of AI algorithms and provide detailed documentation on functionality and risks. Section 7 grants users the right to receive explanations about AI system algorithms.
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).

fr1_title: Resolution of the Politburo on breakthroughs in education and training development (Resolution No. 71-NQ/TW)
fr1_justif: The document stresses the importance of redefining a new vision and strategy for the education system that contextualizes digital technology and artificial intelligence (P12). It set the objective of II.1 (p3) pursuing universalization of technological capacity artificial intelligence and English proficiency at the 12-K school level. This objective shall be achieved by III.4 (p7) Comprehensive universal digital transformation and strong application of digital technology and artificial intelligence in education and training; 7 (p10) Strongly renovating training programs according to international standards; integrate content on data analytics and artificial intelligence and entrepreneurship.
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_body_name: Party Committee of the National Assembly

fr1_title: Draft Law on Artificial Intelligence (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: AI-facilitated Misinformation and Violence is listed as one of the prohibited cases of AI applications with the potential of causing serious harm to social order safety and national security (Art 11.7 p7). Therefore AI-facilitated Misinformation and Violence is perceived as a threat to information security and social order security and lies under the responsibility of the Ministry of Public Security (Art 66.2 p36). Moreover AI-facilitated Misinformation and Violence is also referred in the context of AI applications in journalism publishing media and cultural and social fields as AI-generated content that violates the law and lies within the monitoring responsibility of the Ministry of Culture Sports and Tourism (Art 66.3 p36).
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_defence_and_security_justif: As mentioned in Article 2.1 (page 1) this Draft Law applies to activities related to artificial intelligence systems taking place in the territory of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam or having artificial intelligence system products and services that affect the market users national security social order and safety and the legitimate rights and interests of Vietnamese organizations and individuals. The only explicit exclusion regards AI applications developed deployed and used exclusively for defense security and intelligence purposes (in Article 2.3 page 1)

fr1_title: Draft Law on Artificial Intelligence (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: Article 60 (p33). Liability for compensation for damages in the artificial intelligence value chain. 1. Organizations and individuals who violate the law in artificial intelligence activities and cause damage to the legitimate rights and interests of other organizations and individuals must compensate according to the provisions of the Civil Code and the provisions of this Chapter. Article 61 (p34). Liability for damages in the artificial intelligence value chain. Article 62 (p3435). Civil liability in cases where artificial intelligence systems cause damage. Article 63. Right to request the Court to force the disclosure of evidence. (pages 33-35)
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_defence_and_security_justif: As mentioned in Article 2.1 (page 1) this Draft Law applies to activities related to artificial intelligence systems taking place in the territory of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam or having artificial intelligence system products and services that affect the market users national security social order and safety and the legitimate rights and interests of Vietnamese organizations and individuals. The only explicit exclusion regards AI applications developed deployed and used exclusively for defense security and intelligence purposes (in Article 2.3 page 1)

fr1_title: A national program on 'Protecting and supporting children's healthy and creative interaction in the online environment 2021 - 2025' (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: The document outlined a comprehensive package of activities to protect children's rights in cyberspace. As for AI specifically the document mentioned the need to establish CSAM (Child Sexual Abuse Material) database to contribute to research and monitoring of negative AI applications in this regard.
fr1_scope: Vertical approach (the framework targets specific sectors specific types of data or  AI technologies.
fr1_body_name: There are many entities in the implementation plan as listed above

fr1_title: Draft Law on Artificial Intelligence (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: The document points out the government’s emphasis on prioritizing AI solutions and products tailored for the Vietnamese context (especially in mainstreaming Vietnamese and other ethnic minorities languages in AI solutions and products as mentioned in Art 35.2.c investment as in Art 38.3.a and impact assessment as in Art 45.3.d)
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_defence_and_security_justif: As mentioned in Article 2.1 this Draft Law applies to activities related to artificial intelligence systems taking place in the territory of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam or having artificial intelligence system products and services that affect the market users national security social order and safety and the legitimate rights and interests of Vietnamese organizations and individuals. The only explicit exclusion regards AI applications developed deployed and used exclusively for defense security and intelligence purposes (in Article 2.3)

fr1_title: Draft Law on Artificial Intelligence (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: Article 4. Basic Principles: All activities related to artificial intelligence systems in Vietnam must comply with the following basic principles: 4. Inclusive and Sustainable Development: Linking artificial intelligence development with the goal of sustainable socio-economic development ensuring fairness and opportunities for all people leaving no one behind protecting the environment and preserving national cultural identity ((page 3 Art 4.4). Article 25. Principles of infrastructure development and ensuring national artificial intelligence sovereignty. d) Sustainable development prioritizing clean energy energy saving and environmental protection (page 16 Art 25.2.d)
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_defence_and_security_justif: As mentioned in Article 2.1 (p1) this Draft Law applies to activities related to artificial intelligence systems taking place in the territory of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam or having artificial intelligence system products and services that affect the market users national security social order and safety and the legitimate rights and interests of Vietnamese organizations and individuals. The only explicit exclusion regards AI applications developed deployed and used exclusively for defense security and intelligence purposes (in Article 2.3 p1)

fr1_title: Draft Law on Artificial Intelligence (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: The document refers to Fairness and Non-discrimination as a key principle that all activities related to AI systems in Vietnam must comply with (Article 4.2&4.4 p.3). Article 11 (p6) lists prohibited AI cases including (3) 'Large-scale social credit scoring of individuals by state agencies leading to unfair or disadvantageous treatment in unrelated social contexts'. Art 44 (p25) emphasized this responsibility in AI applications to provide public services. Art 45.3 (p26) obliges AI systems classified as high-risks or having systemic risks to undertake impact assessments on fairness and discriminatory bias. Administrative penalties can be imposed(Art 56.2.b p.32) & also a subject of state inspection Art 54 p.31
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_defence_and_security_justif: As mentioned in Article 2.1 (page 1) this Draft Law applies to activities related to artificial intelligence systems taking place in the territory of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam or having artificial intelligence system products and services that affect the market users national security social order and safety and the legitimate rights and interests of Vietnamese organizations and individuals. The only explicit exclusion regards AI applications developed deployed and used exclusively for defense security and intelligence purposes (in Article 2.3 page 1)

fr1_title: Draft Law on Artificial Intelligence (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: The document establishes multiple safeguards ensuring human oversight of AI systems. Article 4(1) sets a human-centered principle requiring AI to support humans remain under human supervision and not replace humans in important decisions (p.3). Article 15 requires providers of high-risk AI systems to establish mechanisms for human monitoring intervention and control ensuring final human decision-making authority where required (Art. 15(4) p.10). In the public sector Article 44 prohibits fully automated decisions that affect individuals’ rights and requires final decisions to remain subject to human supervision and accountability (Art. 44(2) p.25).
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_defence_and_security_justif: As mentioned in Article 2.1 (page 1) this Draft Law applies to activities related to artificial intelligence systems taking place in the territory of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam or having artificial intelligence system products and services that affect the market users national security social order and safety and the legitimate rights and interests of Vietnamese organizations and individuals. The only explicit exclusion regards AI applications developed deployed and used exclusively for defense security and intelligence purposes (in Article 2.3 page 1)

fr1_title: Draft Law on Artificial Intelligence (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: The document establishes a formal Artificial Intelligence Impact Assessment requirement. Article 45 defines it as a process for identifying analyzing predicting and mitigating potential negative impacts of AI systems on human rights and social values (Art. 45(1)). Developers or deployers of high-risk AI systems or systemic-risk models must conduct the assessment before market release or use (Art. 45(2)). The report must evaluate impacts on human rights privacy equality safety and risks of bias or discrimination and propose mitigation measures including protections for vulnerable groups (Art. 45(3)(a–d)). The government will issue detailed procedures and templates (Art. 45(4)).
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_defence_and_security_justif: As mentioned in Article 2.1 (page 1) this Draft Law applies to activities related to artificial intelligence systems taking place in the territory of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam or having artificial intelligence system products and services that affect the market users national security social order and safety and the legitimate rights and interests of Vietnamese organizations and individuals. The only explicit exclusion regards AI applications developed deployed and used exclusively for defense security and intelligence purposes (in Article 2.3 page 1)

indicator: Public Disclosure of Government Algorithmic Systems
fr1_title: Draft Law on Artificial Intelligence (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: The document considers Public Disclosure of Gov Algorithmic Systems a top mandatory responsibility and gov agencies must embrace the leadership in setting a bright example in ensuring transparency and accountability when applying AI systems (Art 44.1 p25). The document stresses the far-reaching impacts of public services delivery and administrative decisions and consequently obliges gov agencies to conduct AI Impact Assessment disclose information about the purpose and scope of AI applications in the public sector and most importantly establish a mechanism for receiving feedback and complaints from citizens which is not even an obligation for private companies (Art 44.3 & 4 p25).
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_defence_and_security_justif: As mentioned in Article 2.1 (page 1) this Draft Law applies to activities related to artificial intelligence systems taking place in the territory of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam or having artificial intelligence system products and services that affect the market users national security social order and safety and the legitimate rights and interests of Vietnamese organizations and individuals. The only explicit exclusion regards AI applications developed deployed and used exclusively for defense security and intelligence purposes (in Article 2.3 page 1)

fr1_title: Decree No. 214/2025/ND-CP detailing a number of articles and measures to implement the bidding law in contractor selection (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: Public procurement of AI in this document lies under the umbrella of bidding packages in strategic fields projects and tasks of scientific research technology special innovation digital transformation (Article 78.2.d 78.3 78.5d.d) and is able to go through simplified process of direct appointment of contractor (p85-59). While AI is not explicitly mentioned AI is one of the regulated digital technologies (Art 78.3.b) which was later specified in Decision No. 1131/QÐ-TTg issuing the List of strategic technologies and strategic technological products on 12th june 2025 by Prime Minister. Translation of the Decision here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_PYYSQ_-QS23rLODo9gB6C7JJS3RxORhpU9UiSkcaS0/edit?usp=sharing
fr1_scope: Vertical approach (the framework targets specific sectors specific types of data or  AI technologies.

fr1_title: National Strategy on Research Development and Application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to 2030 (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: The document perceives Public Sector Skills Development as one of top priorities in the strategic development of AI in Vietnam (Section III.4 p5). Since the document dated back to 2021 before the bloom of AI with chatGPT the major focus of Public Sector Skills Development is to raise awareness about AI applications; build basic-level AI skills for government officials; and shift management and administration mindsets to adapt to the age of AI integration (Section IV.2 p8)
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_body_name: Ministry of Education and Training Ministry of Information and Communications and the Ministry of Labour Invalids and Social Affairs

fr1_title: National Strategy on Research Development and Application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to 2030 (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: The strategy promotes workforce upskilling and reskilling in AI through training certification and career-transition programs. It calls for implementing and popularizing basic skills in AI and data science to promote innovation among young people and supports short-term and medium-term certification training in AI for students and workers seeking career changes (Strategic Orientation – Human Resource Development pp.4–5). It also encourages short-term AI training programs for research technical and management staff (Ministry of Information and Communications tasks p.7) and provides AI and data science counseling and training for workers changing careers (Ministry of Labour tasks p.11).
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_body_name: Ministry of Education and Training Ministry of Information and Communications and the Ministry of Labour Invalids and Social Affairs

fr1_title: Draft Law on Artificial Intelligence (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: The document refers to Safety and Security as one of the key principles that all activities related to artificial intelligence systems in Vietnam must comply with. Specifically Article 4.2 (page 3) says 'Ensuring Safety Fairness Transparency and Responsibility: Artificial intelligence systems must be developed and operated in a safe reliable secure and fair and non-discriminatory manner. For artificial intelligence systems with risks it is important to ensure transparency accountability and clearly define legal responsibilities for damages caused.'
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
fr1_defence_and_security_justif: As mentioned in Article 2.1 (page 1) this Draft Law applies to activities related to artificial intelligence systems taking place in the territory of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam or having artificial intelligence system products and services that affect the market users national security social order and safety and the legitimate rights and interests of Vietnamese organizations and individuals. The only explicit exclusion regards AI applications developed deployed and used exclusively for defense security and intelligence purposes (in Article 2.3 page 1)

fr1_title: Draft Law on Artificial Intelligence (Vietnam)
fr1_justif: The document refers to Transparency and Explainability as a key principle that all activities related to AI systems in Vietnam must comply with (Article 4.2 p.3). The Court can enforce transparency (Art 63 p.35). Administrative penalties can be imposed in case of violating transparency obligations (Art 56.2.b p.32). Transparency and Explainability also becomes a subject of state inspection and auditing activities in the field of AI in accordance with the Law on Inspection and relevant laws (Art 54 p.31) shall align with international standards and practices regarding responsible artificial intelligence (Art 53 p.30) a vital principle for AI systems in the public sect (Art44p25) & policy implementation (Art 39p23).
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).

The only explicit exclusion regards AI applications developed deployed and used exclusively for defense security and intelligence purposes (in Article 2.3 page 1)

fr1_title: National Artificial Intelligence Strategy 2024 - 2026 (Zambia)
fr1_justif: The Strategy defines AI Literacy (Page 5) as “understanding and competence in AI enabling citizens and professionals to effectively engage with and use AI technologies.” The Executive Summary (Page 11) states that “AI literacy campaigns will be launched to raise awareness and empower citizens to participate in the AI economy.” Further Section 6 (Page 34) calls to “address the digital divide by expanding connectivity and digital literacy programs.” Together these show Zambia’s plan to promote nationwide AI awareness skills and equitable access to AI education. Overall the aim is to ensure citizens are knowledgeable in using AI and can actively benefit from opportunities in the evolving digital economy.
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).

fr1_title: National Artificial Intelligence Strategy 2024 - 2026 (Zambia)
fr1_justif: Page 29 of the Zambia National AI Strategy under the Ethical and Responsible AI theme explicitly lists Fairness and Non-discrimination as a core principle. It emphasizes that AI systems must be designed to prevent biases ensuring equitable treatment of all individuals regardless of race gender ethnicity or socio-economic status. The Strategy on page 13 further recommends that in Preventing Bias and Discrimination Implementing measures to identify and minimise biases in AI algorithms ensuring fairness and equity in AI-driven decisions should be taken
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).

fr1_title: National Artificial Intelligence Strategy 2024 - 2026 (Zambia)
fr1_justif: The AI Strategy (2024–2026) promotes human oversight through its emphasis on ethical inclusive and human-centric AI as noted on section 5.2.1 “Ethical and Responsible AI” as well as pages 18–20 highlight the need for transparency accountability and fairness to ensure human control and prevent harm. It calls for governance structures like the National AI Council to oversee AI deployment and maintain human decision-making in critical areas aligning with AU and UNESCO principles on human oversight as noted on page 13-14 section 2.2 Ethical Commitment and International Alignment. Additionally page 41 on numbering 4 states under Ethical Oversight Advocate for ethical considerations and the protection of human rights in AI deployment.
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).

fr1_title: The Data Protection Act No. 3 of 2021 (Zambia)
fr1_justif: The Act addresses Impact Assessments as they relate to AI through rules on automated processing profiling new technologies and high-risk processing. Part VIII Section 46 requires a Data Protection Impact Assessment before processing when new technologies such as AI as they pose high risk. Section 46(2)(a) explicitly covers automated processing systems including profiling which produce legal or significant effects making these AI-related activities subject to mandatory Impact Assessments.
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).

fr1_title: National Digital Transformation Strategy 2023-2027 (Zambia)
fr1_justif: The strategy highlights the goal of training 5000 ICT professionals in new and emerging technologies including artificial intelligence (AI) robotics machine learning blockchain and big data (Page 31 Row 9 in the table). This initiative aims to address the skills gap and enhance workforce readiness for the digital economy. The focus on AI and related technologies underscores the government's commitment to upskilling and reskilling programs to equip professionals with the competencies needed to drive Zambia’s digital transformation.
fr1_scope: Horizontal approach (the framework applies across multiple sectors or applications and is not limited to a particular domain or  AI technology type).
